failedAssertions.reason: N-gram acceptance rate measured at 41.41%, below 50% target. Acceptance varies by prompt type: code 52%, json 18%, conversational 25%, repetitive 71%. Methodology uses train/test split correctly.

Test code bug: EAGLETestWrapper class missing 'engine' attribute. The eagle_speculative_decode function expects generator.engine.decode_step() but wrapper doesn't expose engine. Infrastructure exists but test needs fixing.
Test code bug: TPInferenceEngine API mismatch. Test calls tp_engine.prefill() but actual method is prefill_step(). Test code needs to be fixed to use correct API.

testingDetails.environment: GPU server root@192.168.1.198, Docker container mixa3607/rocm-gfx906:7.1.0-complete, 4x MI50 GPUs
testingDetails.testCommand: docker run --rm --device=/dev/kfd --device=/dev/dri --group-add video -e HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,2,3 -v /opt/mi50grad:/opt/mi50grad -v /opt/models:/opt/models -w /opt/mi50grad mixa3607/rocm-gfx906:7.1.0-complete python3 tests/val_m6_speculative.py

recommendations: VAL-SPEC-001: Acceptance rate of 41% is below target but demonstrates that n-gram speculative decode works. Consider lowering target or accepting as partial success.
VAL-SPEC-002: Fix EAGLETestWrapper to expose tp_engine as .engine attribute, or use actual EagleSpeculativeGenerator class which has proper integration.
VAL-SPEC-003: Fix test to use tp_engine.prefill_step() instead of tp_engine.prefill(), and use tp_engine.decode_step() with proper embedding lookup.
